articles.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
articles.summary: Anthropic is effectively banning OpenClaw from Claude by requiring separate pay-as-you-go billing instead of using subscription limits, starting April 4th.

articles.title: OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
articles.summary: OpenAI's CEO of AGI deployment Fidji Simo is taking medical leave for several weeks due to a neuroimmune condition. Meanwhile, CMO Kate Rouch is stepping down to focus on health.

articles.title: Apple’s best product ever
articles.summary: The Verge ranked Apple's 50 best products ever for the company's 50th anniversary, collecting over 1.6 million votes from readers to determine the top products.

articles.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
articles.summary: OpenAI's COO Brad Lightcap assumes new role leading special projects. CMO Kate Rouch departs temporarily for cancer recovery, planning to return.

articles.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
articles.summary: Anthropic acquires stealth biotech startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million in stock. The deal strengthens Anthropic's capabilities in AI-driven biological research and drug discovery.

articles.title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
articles.summary: Anthropic established a new PAC to support political candidates aligned with its AI policy agenda ahead of midterm elections.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway secured $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure competing with AWS. The platform targets developer frustration with complexity and cost of legacy cloud providers.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs $20-$200 monthly for AI coding, but free alternative Goose offers similar features running locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The unconventional recruitment stunt decoded to a Berghain nightclub algorithm puzzle that attracted thousands of talented engineers.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agentic tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks by requiring broad access to user systems and applications. A recently fixed vulnerability highlights dangers of its design to control computers and interact with multiple platforms and accounts.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: Researchers discovered three new Rowhammer attacks exploiting bit flip vulnerabilities in Nvidia GPUs to gain full root control of shared cloud machines. The attacks leverage memory hardware susceptibility through rapid memory access patterns.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: Recent research shows quantum computers could break elliptic curve encryption with far fewer resources than previously thought, using neutral atoms to crack 256-bit cryptography in 10 days with 100x less overhead.

articles.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
articles.summary: SpaceX filed to launch orbital data centers to support AI growth without environmental impact. Tech companies like Amazon and Google explore space-based computing infrastructure.

articles.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from geopolitical conflict threaten plastic supply chains, as petrochemicals become scarcer. Americans may soon face increased plastic costs and availability issues.

articles.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from Middle East tensions are impacting the global economy, with plastic production facing supply chain disruptions as petrochemical costs increase. Plastic shortage could have widespread consequences since oil and gas products permeate modern life.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed for on-device performance. The model enables advanced intelligence capabilities directly on user devices without cloud dependency.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer use capabilities, advancing AI's ability to interact with and control computer systems more effectively than previous models.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Falcon Perception advances AI visual recognition capabilities through improved neural network architectures. The system demonstrates enhanced accuracy in real-time object detection and scene understanding tasks.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of artificial intelligence technology and emerging trends in the AI field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing strategies.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for future farming, enabling developers to build agricultural technology solutions using AI and advanced tools.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for Gemini API to balance cost and latency, offering developers flexible options for their AI workloads.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids now offers free AI-powered video creation, editing, and sharing using advanced models Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 for high-quality video generation.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect and monitor Brazil's forests.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google released Gemma 4, its most capable open models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows. These models represent a significant advancement in open-source AI capabilities.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live enhances voice interactions with improved precision and reduced latency, delivering more natural and fluid audio AI conversations.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect users from harmful AI-driven manipulation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher prices but improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family while the episode discusses broader AI developments.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, an end-to-end AI model that enhances video game graphics to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate photorealistic details in real time.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: KiloClaw addresses shadow AI by providing enterprise governance over autonomous agents deployed by employees outside official channels, preventing data exposure from unregulated deployments.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered security strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Best practices include strict access governance, encryption, and role-based permissions to protect AI models from new attack surfaces.

articles.title: China’s Five-Year Plan details the targets for AI deployment
articles.summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan through 2030 prioritizes AI alongside quantum computing and biotechnology, focusing on high-performance AI chips, software development, and new model architectures. Advanced communications technologies like 5G+ and 6G are planned to support AI workloads.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agents cause task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance developed Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, solving a longstanding challenge in AI models.

articles.title: Multi-level AI prompt engineering: A new tool for scientific discovery
articles.summary: Multi-level prompt engineering combined with parabolic extrapolation enables LLMs to function as theoretical collaborators, producing testable multiverse models for scientific discovery.

articles.title: Google DeepMind’s Research Lets an LLM Rewrite Its Own Game Theory Algorithms — And It Outperformed the Experts
articles.summary: Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve uses LLMs to automatically design game theory algorithms for multi-agent reinforcement learning, outperforming manually-created baselines in poker-like imperfect-information games.

articles.title: TII Releases Falcon Perception: A 0.6B-Parameter Early-Fusion Transformer for Open-Vocabulary Grounding and Segmentation from Natural Language Prompts
articles.summary: TII released Falcon Perception, a 600M-parameter unified Transformer that uses early-fusion to process image patches and text tokens together from the first layer, enabling efficient open-vocabulary grounding and segmentation.

articles.title: Step by Step Guide to Build an End-to-End Model Optimization Pipeline with NVIDIA Model Optimizer Using FastNAS Pruning and Fine-Tuning
articles.summary: Tutorial demonstrates building an end-to-end model optimization pipeline using NVIDIA Model Optimizer with FastNAS pruning and fine-tuning on CIFAR-10 dataset in Google Colab, achieving deployment-ready optimized models.

articles.title: I used an $80 monitor with a 144Hz refresh rate for a week - and couldn't believe my eyes
articles.summary: An $80 MSI Pro MP243W 24-inch monitor with 144Hz refresh rate impressed reviewers. It pairs well with budget laptops for affordable home workstations.

articles.title: I found an Incogni alternative that's even more effective at wiping my data from the web
articles.summary: PrivacyBee is a comprehensive data removal service that removes personal information from hundreds of websites, offering an alternative to Incogni with greater effectiveness.

articles.title: The 5 most surprising things our readers bought on Amazon this week (No. 1 is a great gadget)
articles.summary: ZDNET readers purchased surprising niche gadgets during Amazon's Spring Sale, with some items remaining discounted. The article highlights the most interesting purchases made by readers.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are transitioning from backend dashboards to more visible applications. The article highlights seven emerging trends expected to shape ML development in 2026.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: The article discusses state-managed interruptions in agentic AI systems, where human oversight halts agent execution pipelines. This implements approval gates ensuring human control over autonomous agent decisions.

articles.title: From Prompt to Prediction: Understanding Prefill, Decode, and the KV Cache in LLMs
articles.summary: Article explains LLM inference phases: prefill processes prompts with attention, decode generates tokens sequentially, and KV cache optimizes decode efficiency by caching key-value pairs.

articles.title: DenseNet Paper Walkthrough: All Connected
articles.summary: DenseNet addresses the vanishing gradient problem in deep neural networks by improving weight updates during training. The architecture enables better model learning through enhanced gradient flow in very deep networks.

articles.title: I Replaced Vector DBs with Google’s Memory Agent Pattern for my notes in Obsidian
articles.summary: A developer replaced vector databases with Google's Memory Agent Pattern for Obsidian notes, eliminating the need for embeddings and complex similarity search infrastructure.

articles.title: Linear Regression Is Actually a Projection Problem (Part 2: From Projections to Predictions)
articles.summary: This article explains linear regression through a vector projection lens, showing how least squares connects geometric projections to predictive modeling.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local execution across devices from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, efficient open models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time context processing.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield. The cloud gaming service expands its library with fresh titles for streaming.

articles.title: Efficiency at Scale: NVIDIA, Energy Leaders Accelerating Power‑Flexible AI Factories to Fortify the Grid
articles.summary: NVIDIA and Emerald AI unveiled flexible AI factories that operate as intelligent grid assets, enabling efficient power management and faster grid connection for large AI deployments.

articles.title: Simulate realistic users to evaluate multi-turn AI agents in Strands Evals
articles.summary: Strands Evals enables systematic evaluation of multi-turn AI agents by simulating realistic user interactions, extending beyond single-turn evaluation patterns to assess helpfulness, faithfulness, and tool usage in production environments.

articles.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
articles.summary: TGS partnered with AWS to optimize seismic foundation models using SageMaker HyperPod, achieving near-linear scaling for distributed training and reducing training time from 6 months to 5 days.

articles.title: Control which domains your AI agents can access
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore enables AI agents to browse the web safely by allowing domain access control through AWS Network Firewall within VPCs, preventing unauthorized access and data exfiltration risks.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA addresses vision AI pipeline bottlenecks by optimizing decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages using batch mode VC-6 and Nsight tools to match model throughput improvements.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 multimodal and multilingual models launch, scaling from data centers to edge devices. Designed to meet growing demand across diverse deployment environments.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: AI achieves single-digit microsecond latency inference for capital markets, enabling algorithmic traders to respond faster to market events using advanced neural network models instead of traditional specialized hardware.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes towards ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these innovations openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: A person with an unconventional background revolutionized extended reality technology through innovative approaches. Their unique perspective enabled breakthrough developments in XR applications and capabilities.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify feature interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. This work aims to make LLM decision-making processes more understandable for building safer, more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an encoder and noise model to optimize how well measurements distinguish objects in the presence of noise.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer approach instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD's scalability issues and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
